pattern: Because each row and column is non-increasing, a staircase walk from one corner crosses the negative boundary exactly once, counting negatives in linear time.
hints: Every row is sorted, so within a row the negatives form one unbroken suffix; you only need where that suffix begins.
The column ordering means the boundary between non-negative and negative drifts monotonically as you move down the rows — it never moves back.
Start at the top-right corner. If the current cell is negative, every cell below it in this column is too; step left. Otherwise step down. Accumulate the negatives as the boundary slides.

edge_inputs.description: mixed grid with a diagonal negative boundary

stress.description: random matrix made non-increasing both ways by sorting, with values spanning zero
